hero.title: From toy syntax to usable language.
hero.subtitle: A practical map: stabilize what exists, make examples runnable, grow stdlib, then improve tooling and runtime. No fake version theater.

rail.title: Make the language easy to learn

sections.title: Learning surface
sections.subtitle: The website should teach real MKS with runnable examples before anything else.

cards.title: Cookbook docs
cards.description: Turn reference snippets into practical recipes people can paste and modify.

cards.title: Examples expansion
cards.description: Examples should cover the same behavior the interpreter tests cover.

cards.title: Truth labels
cards.description: Document what is tested, what is experimental, and what should not be relied on yet.

sections.title: Standard library UX
sections.subtitle: Make stdlib feel like part of the language, not hidden files in the repo.

cards.title: Stdlib explorer
cards.description: A searchable page for std/math, std/array, std/string, std/bool, and std/time.

cards.title: Array/string recipes
cards.description: Practical examples for common data work.

cards.title: Import clarity
cards.description: Make module loading behavior obvious and avoid alias confusion until runtime supports it.

sections.title: Interactive workflow
sections.subtitle: Move from reading examples to running and sharing them.

cards.title: Open in Playground
cards.description: Every example card should open directly in the playground.

cards.title: Visual trace mode
cards.description: Explain simple snippets step by step without pretending to be the full interpreter.

sections.title: Tooling and diagnostics
sections.subtitle: After docs and stdlib are understandable, make daily use smoother.

cards.title: Better errors
cards.description: Parser/runtime errors should point to fixes faster.

cards.title: Formatter
cards.description: One official code style for arrow blocks, entity, extend, and loops.

cards.title: Test runner polish
cards.description: Make built-in test blocks useful as a workflow.

sections.title: Runtime strength
sections.subtitle: Runtime work matters, but only after the surface is understandable.

cards.title: Method consistency
cards.description: Make native and extended methods feel predictable.

cards.title: Performance passes
cards.description: Tighten evaluator and environment behavior when user-facing semantics settle.

cards.title: Compiler experiments
cards.description: Only worth chasing once syntax, stdlib, docs, and tooling are less wobbly.

timeline.title: Useful milestones
timeline.subtitle: Each step should make the site or language easier to use.

items.title: Docs as cookbook
items.text: Focus on loops, modules, stdlib, entities, tests, and real examples.

items.title: Stdlib explorer
items.text: Expose std/math, std/array, std/string, std/bool, and std/time with examples.

items.title: Playground flow
items.text: Open examples in playground and add presets.

items.title: Tooling polish
items.text: Formatter, clearer diagnostics, and better test runner.

items.title: Runtime/compiler work
items.text: Performance and compiler experiments after the surface settles.

principle.title: Make useful things obvious.
principle.text: MKS is experimental, so the site should teach what actually works, mark uncertainty, and prioritize runnable snippets over internal project noise.
